hero.badge: TikTok lyric video generator — vertical, AI-powered, free to export
hero.h1: TikTok Lyric Video Generator — Vertical Lyrics That Stay Readable
hero.subhead: Upload your song audio. AI transcribes the lyrics and syncs every line to the beat. Preview in a vertical 9:16 frame with TikTok safe zones, then export — free, no subscription needed. One render works for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts.

howItWorks.title: How the TikTok Lyric Video Generator Works

steps.title: Upload your song
steps.description: Drop an MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, or M4A file (up to 100MB). No account needed to start. The TikTok lyric video generator works from audio, so you do not need to type a single lyric line. Pick the 15-to-30-second section that will stop the scroll, or use the full track.

steps.title: AI transcribes and syncs every line
steps.description: Lyrics are transcribed automatically from the audio and synced to the beat — in 50+ languages. Click any line to edit. The TikTok lyric video maker positions text inside the vertical safe zone so your words are never buried under TikTok's like, comment, and share buttons.

steps.title: Preview vertically and export in 9:16
steps.description: Watch the draft on a phone-sized canvas before you post. Confirm the first lyric lands within 2 seconds, text is large enough for a 6-inch screen, and nothing is hidden by TikTok UI. Export in 9:16 HD — the same file works on TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.

whyChoose.title: Why Make TikTok Lyric Videos on LyricVideoMaker
whyChoose.description: Most lyric video tools are built for YouTube — horizontal, full-length, desktop-first. LyricVideoMaker is the TikTok lyric video generator built for vertical, short-form, phone-first content from the start.

highlight.title: Vertical-first, not vertical-as-an-afterthought
highlight.description: Every preview, every text layout, and every export is designed around a 9:16 phone screen with TikTok safe zones. You are not cropping a horizontal video — you are building a vertical one from scratch.

cards.title: Phone-screen lyric sizing
cards.description: Lyrics are sized for a vertical phone feed, not squeezed down from a widescreen lyric video. The result stays readable when someone watches with one thumb on the screen.

cards.title: TikTok safe zones built in
cards.description: Lyrics are automatically positioned away from the like, comment, share buttons, and caption area. No more guessing whether your text will be readable after posting.

cards.title: Short-form pacing by default
cards.description: The first lyric appears fast, hook clips stay tight, and endings feel loop-friendly. The page is built for TikTok behavior instead of full-song playback only.

contentSections.heading: What is a TikTok lyric video generator?
contentSections.intro: A TikTok lyric video generator creates a vertical, phone-readable lyric video from your song audio. It is built for 9:16 viewing, quick hooks, and safe lyric placement, so the words do not fight TikTok captions, buttons, or tiny cropped text.
bullets: Input is an audio file from your song; link importing is a future convenience, not the stable path.
It can transcribe vocals, sync lines to the beat, and build vertical AI scenes around the hook.
Output is a 9:16 MP4, with 16:9 and 1:1 versions available when you need them.
It differs from cropping a YouTube lyric video because layout, pacing, and safe zones are vertical-first.

contentSections.heading: TikTok lyric video generator specs at a glance

rows: Accepted input
Audio upload only: MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, or M4A, up to 100MB per file.
Lyrics
AI transcribes vocals automatically in 50+ languages, then lets you edit every line before export.
Sync
Beat-aware line timing keeps lyrics readable and musical instead of simply dropping captions on a fixed grid.
Visuals
Storyboard planning and AI scene-image generation create backgrounds around the mood of the song.
Exports
MP4 with original audio in 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 for YouTube, Shorts, TikTok, Reels, and feeds.
Start
Free credits are available at the start, so you can preview the workflow before spending more time on a release.

contentSections.heading: TikTok lyric video safe-zone planning
contentSections.intro: A TikTok lyric video lives under interface elements: captions at the bottom, action buttons on the right, profile and audio metadata near the lower edge. The design should treat those areas as occupied before the video is rendered.

rows: Right-side buttons
Keep key words away from the right edge so likes, comments, and shares do not cover the hook.
Bottom caption area
Place main lyric lines above the caption zone, especially when you plan a long post description.
First two seconds
Bring in either the title or the first memorable lyric immediately; silent visual intros are risky.
Loop behavior
Avoid a hard visual stop at the end of a short clip; a soft return encourages replay.
Text size
Use fewer words per line and larger type than a YouTube version would need.
Cross-posting
A 9:16 render can usually serve TikTok, Reels, and Shorts if the safe zone is conservative.

contentSections.heading: Tips for TikTok lyric videos that survive the scroll
contentSections.intro: Use these checks before you render, especially when the same project needs more than one platform format.
bullets: Choose the most quotable 15 to 30 seconds instead of uploading the full song by default.
Keep one lyric idea per screen; fast stacked lines turn into wallpaper on a phone.
Let the chorus or hook have a stronger visual treatment than the verse so the clip feels shareable.
Use high contrast between text and generated scene art; TikTok compression punishes subtle color differences.
Preview with interface overlays in mind, not in a clean desktop player.
Export a square or widescreen companion only after the vertical version reads well.
Write the caption after seeing the generated mood, because the visuals may reveal a better angle for the post.

contentSections.heading: Why TikTok lyric videos need their own layout logic
body: A TikTok lyric video generator creates a vertical, phone-readable lyric video from your song audio. It is built for 9:16 viewing, quick hooks, and safe lyric placement, so the words do not fight TikTok captions, buttons, or tiny cropped text.

A TikTok lyric video lives under interface elements: captions at the bottom, action buttons on the right, profile and audio metadata near the lower edge. The design should treat those areas as occupied before the video is rendered.
Use these checks before you render a TikTok lyric video generator, especially when the video will be reused across several platforms.

The mistake most artists make is treating TikTok as a crop of the official lyric video. A widescreen design can look polished on YouTube and still fail on a phone because every line becomes too small, the hook arrives too late, and the app UI sits exactly where the words should be.

Starting vertical changes the creative decision. The generator can size lyrics for thumb-distance viewing, pace cuts around the hook, and leave quiet space for the UI. The result feels native to short-form instead of like a repurposed asset.

TikTok is a full-screen, sound-on, phone-first feed, and a horizontal lyric video shrunk into that space wastes most of the screen. A vertical 9:16 lyric video fills the frame, puts the words at eye level, and reads instantly with one thumb on the screen — which is exactly how people scroll.

A good TikTok lyric video generator also protects the listener's attention. The viewer should never have to choose between reading the line and watching the scene; the scene exists to make the lyric feel more alive. That is why contrast, line length, timing, and motion restraint matter as much as the first generated image.

The review habit is simple: watch once for words, once for rhythm, and once for platform fit. The first pass catches transcription mistakes, the second catches lines that arrive early or late, and the third catches text that works on desktop but fails on a phone or inside a social feed.

For short-form release clips, the real win is not only speed. It is having one source project that can become a full video, a short vertical clip, and a square feed asset without retyping lyrics or rebuilding timing. Consistency across those exports makes a release feel planned.

The safest publishing pattern is to make the lyric video while the song is still fresh in your ear. You will hear awkward line breaks faster, recognize misheard words sooner, and make better choices about where the visual energy should rise or stay quiet.

The useful boundary is that AI should remove repetitive labor, not remove taste. Let it transcribe, sync, and propose scenes; then use the preview to decide whether the song needs more space, more contrast, a calmer verse, or a stronger hook treatment.

That is also why the audio-first constraint is helpful. Starting with a clean song file keeps the workflow focused on vocals, beat, and structure before any visual decoration appears. Once those foundations are correct, the design choices become much easier to judge.

contentSections.heading: TikTok lyric video generator checklist before export
contentSections.intro: A quick pre-export review catches most quality problems while they are still cheap to fix.

rows: Audio readiness
Accepted formats are MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, and M4A; keep the file under 100MB.
Language review
The system auto-detects 50+ languages, but bilingual hooks and names deserve a manual pass.
Timing review
Beat sync gives a strong baseline; nudge any line that lands early or late against the vocal.
Scene review
Generated images should match the mood without making text hard to read.
Export review
Check 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 separately because readable text size changes by format.

items.question: How long should a TikTok lyric video be?
items.answer: 15 to 30 seconds performs best. Pick the hook, chorus, or the lyric people would repeat after hearing it once, and set start and end points before exporting.

items.question: How to add song lyrics to TikTok video without typing them?
items.answer: Upload your audio to this TikTok lyric video generator. The AI transcribes and syncs every lyric line automatically — no manual typing needed, in 50+ languages.

items.question: Will the lyrics avoid TikTok's buttons and captions?
items.answer: Yes. All TikTok exports keep lyrics inside the safe zone, away from the like, comment, and share buttons and the caption area.

items.question: Can I post the same video to Reels and Shorts?
items.answer: Yes. The 9:16 export works on TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts. One render, three platforms.

items.question: Is this TikTok lyric video maker free?
items.answer: Yes. Sign up, get free credits, and export your TikTok lyric video — no subscription required and no credit card required.

items.question: Can I use TikTok lyric videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes. Your TikTok lyric videos are yours, including monetized and brand accounts. Just make sure you have the rights to the music you upload.
